verdict_reasoning: SRC (p60-Src) is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase that localizes to the cytoplasmic face of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation, placing its kinase domain entirely on the inner leaflet. It has no extracellular domain and no transmembrane segment. Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented stress/activation-induced exposure on the outer leaflet; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no lineage-specific outer-leaflet display; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — cytoplasmic pools and inner-leaflet PM pools only, no outer-leaflet pool reported; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no secreted form that re-anchors. All therapeutic programs targeting SRC use intracellular small-molecule inhibitors (dasatinib, bosutinib), consistent with cytoplasmic/inner-leaflet localization. Surface biotinylation and flow cytometry on intact non-permeabilized cells do not detect SRC on the cell exterior. Verdict: inner-leaflet anchored, not accessible from the extracellular face.
